meta.title: Request Processing Contract
meta.purpose: Defines the structural rules, patterns, and enforcement logic governing how every request is received, evaluated, and resolved. Not a feature spec — a processing discipline.
meta.scope: Applies to every inbound message at POST /api/v1/session/{id}/message

foundation.description: Every decision in this system is a measurement of difference. When difference falls below a threshold, a gate passes. When it exceeds a threshold, a gate blocks. Similarity is 1 - normalized_difference.

axioms.application: Every enforcement check subtracts an observed value from a limit. The difference determines the outcome.

axioms.statement: similarity = 1.0 - min(1.0, abs(a - b) / max(abs(a), abs(b), 1.0))
axioms.application: Pattern matching, feature comparison, and confidence scoring all derive similarity from difference.
Every difference maps to a gate_type. Unnamed difference is logged as unclassified and flagged for review.

axioms.statement: the response is proportional to the difference
axioms.application: Brief length, session duration, and safety checks all scale with measured difference, not with assumption.

patterns.description: Five structural patterns govern all processing decisions. They are not guidelines — they are enforced shapes.

gate.name: Gate Pattern
gate.statement: Every request passes through a named threshold. Below the threshold: pass. At or above: block.

proportionality.name: Proportionality Pattern
proportionality.statement: Every limit, check, and response scales with tier and risk. Nothing is flat.

instances.hard_cap: scope-dependent output budget in the router

fail_closed.name: Fail-Closed Pattern
fail_closed.statement: When a check throws or produces an ambiguous result, access is denied. Trust is earned, not assumed.
fail_closed.note: Mirrors GRID AdvancedPatternManager DEFAULT_BIAS = -0.5 (~37% base probability). Skeptical by default.

instances.rule: if tier unknown → fall back to trial (strictest limits)
if gate tag missing → gate_type = unclassified, still deliver brief, flag for review
Never trust the client. All enforcement is server-side only.

single_responsibility.name: Single Responsibility Pattern
single_responsibility.statement: Each layer owns exactly one problem. No layer handles another layer's failure.

layers.does_not_own: user text, raw IPs, card data

minimum_necessary.name: Minimum Necessary Pattern
minimum_necessary.statement: Do only what is required to close the difference. Never overstep.

rules.statement: Never complete the task for the user. Unblock them.
Never store user text. Process in memory, discard.
Never store raw IPs. Hash with SHA-256.
Never store card data. Stripe owns it.
Never ask open-ended follow-up questions after the brief.
Never retry a rate-limited provider against the same provider. Fall through to the next.

pipeline.description: The ordered sequence of gates every request must pass. Each gate is a named difference check. Failure at any gate terminates processing at that layer — except where noted as non-blocking.

steps.check: JWT bearer token is valid and not expired
no concurrent request already in progress for this session_id

block.message: Request already in progress for this session.

steps.check: input length <= 2000, no control chars, no prompt injection patterns
steps.note: Control characters are stripped silently. PII is redacted before the message reaches the LLM.

steps.check: llm_call_count < tier.maxLlmCalls AND elapsed < tier.maxDurationMs AND input not empty

empty_input.message: Please describe what you're stuck on.

steps.check: tier-proportional abuse pattern check via fail-closed wrapper

tiers.trial: always passes — low capability, low risk
tiers.continuous: blocks if avg_interval_ms < 5000 across message count

steps.check: selected provider responds within the scope-dependent timeout budget with non-empty content

parameters.output_budget: scope dependent: balanced and fast remain compact; deep_read increases output budget

timeout.message: That took too long. Please try again.

rate_limited.message: The service is busy. Please try again.

empty.fallback_brief: I wasn't able to generate a useful response. Please try rephrasing.

provider_chain.description: Build a routing plan from task type, complexity, and scope. Discover Ollama models, rank candidates, and try them first. Use OpenAI only when explicitly forced or in rare deep-read/high-complexity rescue cases.

request_controls.openai_override: auto | force | never, defaults to auto
request_controls.allow_routing_memory: server derived from stored consent only

provider_chain.structural_note: This is the Gate Pattern applied to infrastructure. Each local model is a candidate. consented routing memory shifts the ranking, not the application logic.

providers.name: ollama
providers.note: Primary provider. Candidate ranking is based on task type, complexity, scope, and consented routing memory.

providers.name: openai_lifeguard
providers.note: Rare rescue path. Used only for forced escalation or rare deep-read/high-complexity rescue.

steps.check: response contains a valid [GATE: type] tag matching known gate_types
steps.block: non-blocking — log gate_type = unclassified, still deliver brief, flag in telemetry
steps.note: Gate classification failure does not break user experience. It signals a prompt quality issue for review.

steps.check: brief <= 150 words, gate tag present, does not end with open-ended question
steps.block: non-blocking — flag in telemetry, still deliver (observation phase only)

steps.check: session state updated, turn recorded, DPR chain stored
steps.pass: response delivered to user, session_lock released
steps.block: non-blocking — fire-and-forget, telemetry failure does not break delivery
steps.invariant: conversation_history is stripped before persisting to Redis. User text is never written to the data layer. See DF-01.

enforcement.rationale: Client cannot override. Server is the sole authority on turns, time, and access.

measurement.description: Four KPIs define what success looks like. Each is a threshold — a gate on system health. The KPI threshold IS the similarity floor. Below it, the system is diverging.

kpis.name: session_success_rate
kpis.action_if_below: alert for investigation after 3 consecutive days below threshold

kpis.name: response_latency
kpis.action_if_above: downgrade prompt complexity or route to a lighter Ollama candidate

kpis.name: context_gate_pass_rate
kpis.action_if_below: review LLM prompt effectiveness after 7 consecutive days below threshold

never: complete the task for the user
retry a rate-limited provider against the same provider
allow a safety check failure to default to open
deliver a response longer than 150 words
ask open-ended follow-up questions after the brief
put API keys in source code or client bundles
skip the session lock before an LLM call

changes: Initial contract. Synthesised from ARCHITECTURE.md, contract.hardened.json, session-controller.ts, safety.ts, safety-pipeline.ts, llm.ts, prompt.ts, and types/session.ts.
Five structural patterns formalised: Gate, Proportionality, Fail-Closed, Single Responsibility, Minimum Necessary.
Provider routing updated to scenario-aware Ollama-first selection with rare OpenAI lifeguard escalation.
Ten-step pipeline grounded in actual enforcement order from POST /api/v1/session/{id}/message.
Foundation axioms derived from GRID cognition/Pattern similarity calculation and chase simulation.
